answers: AR (Augmented Reality) = overlays digital content onto real world. Pokemon Go, Snapchat filters, IKEA furniture app.
AR vs VR: AR adds to reality (you see real world + digital), VR replaces reality entirely. AR is more practical daily.
AR examples: navigation arrows on street, try-on virtual clothes, measure rooms with phone, interactive learning.
AR tech: smartphone AR (most common), AR glasses (Meta, Microsoft HoloLens), and soon lightweight AR contact lenses.
